task_completion.description: Open the structural cross-section schedule to view the required dimensions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window is open displaying the contents of 'i_beam_specification.txt' from the desktop.

task_completion.description: Open the initial I-beam profile in SolveSpace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace application is open and displays the unconstrained asymmetric I-beam sketch ('i_beam_profile.slvs').

task_completion.description: Apply the overall width and height constraints to the sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace canvas shows dimensional constraints for overall width (120) and overall height (90) applied to the I-beam.

task_completion.description: Apply the top and bottom flange thickness constraints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace canvas shows dimensional constraints for bottom flange thickness (12) and top flange thickness (18) applied to the sketch.

task_completion.description: Apply the web and overhang constraints to fully define the sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace canvas shows dimensional constraints for right overhang (40), left overhang (30), web clear height (60), and web thickness (50).

task_completion.description: Save the fully constrained sketch to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog saving the file to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/i_beam_constrained.slvs', or the SolveSpace window title updated to 'i_beam_constrained.slvs'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the SolveSpace GUI to apply constraints.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the SolveSpace interface (menus, toolbars, or constraint tools) rather than editing the raw .slvs file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the dimensions are actual SolveSpace constraints.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dimensions appear as standard SolveSpace constraint annotations (typically purple text with dimension lines), not as separate text objects or image edits.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just copy the input file to the output path without modifying it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must be seen actively applying the constraints in the SolveSpace GUI before saving the final file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/i_beam_constrained.slvs'. The 8 required constraints are: overall flange width (120mm), overall section height (90mm), bottom flange thickness (12mm), top flange thickness (18mm), right flange overhang (40mm), left flange overhang (30mm), web clear height (60mm), and web thickness (50mm). Tolerance is 0.5mm. In SolveSpace, applied dimensional constraints typically appear as purple text with dimension lines.
